column1: Amazing how models will agree when there's actually a center to track!  This is a Big Bend storm. Our coastal areas will get pretty gusty tomorrow with much less rain and wind inland. I'll give updated thoughts after the 11pm NHC update.
Thanks as always for the updates. I hope everyone in Big Bend area is safe.
in other news, I like your surname!
I’m predicting not much rain tomorrow as we head to church
Only a little over 11 months since Idalia tore up Cedar Key and much of the Big Bend area. I hope they don't get a big storm surge and severe flooding, they're still rebuilding from last year.
I know they are still rebuilding from last August. I have been up there. I really hope this is not bad for them.
praying for Cedar Key! Love it there!
we were just there as my parent's have a place right on the water there. Fingers crossed they get through unscathed.
praying for Cedar Key! Love it there! Hope your parents place will be ok!

he says “less rain and wind inland”. The 2 is a typo. Two separate things in one sentence. Maybe he should have made it two sentences, but it is grammatically correct.

I'm not a Florida native, and I'm new to Pasco county, up from Lee county.
I'm not sure if Pasco county, more specifically Hudson Florida is part of the Big Bend area.
I live one mile off of U.S. 19, on the side furthest from the water.
Is that a dangerous area in terms of flooding and wind?
You are not in the Big Bend area. It is way north of you, near the Panhandle. Hope this helps.
So appreciated! 🥰
you should be fine 😊
Hudson is not part of Big Bend. Big Bend is essentially where the Panhandle and the peninsula meet, near Tallahassee area.
Thank you so much! 🥰

if you look at the map the big bend is where you can actually see the “bend” of the state. Kinda like where the Gulf/land turns west along our coast into panhandle. You should be ok from storm surge from gulf but if you have a lake, canal think about that. Google for local flood zone maps.
Good to know, thank you! 🥰
